skill.name: ai-ml
skill.description: AI and machine learning workflow covering LLM application development, RAG implementation, agent architecture, ML pipelines, and AI-powered features.
skill.summary: Comprehensive workflow bundle for building LLM applications, implementing RAG systems, creating AI agents, and developing ML pipelines.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 75 potential issues (external_commands, weak_crypto, system_reconnaissance) but all are false positives. The file is a markdown documentation bundle that orchestrates other skills via reference names in code blocks. No actual code execution, shell commands, or cryptographic operations exist. This is safe for publication.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands Detection
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at 64 locations (lines 32-252). These are markdown code fences (```) containing example prompts like 'Use @ai-product to design AI-powered features', NOT actual shell commands. The backtick syntax is markdown formatting for code blocks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown code blocks with example prompts do not execute. The '@' references are skill invocation patterns, not shell commands. No backticks used for command substitution exist in this file.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at 11 locations (lines 3, 29, 40, 46, 50, 93, 103, 125, 142, 155). These are YAML frontmatter fields like 'risk: safe', 'domain: artificial-intelligence' - configuration metadata, not crypto algorithms.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: YAML frontmatter contains only metadata fields (name, description, risk, domain, category, version). No cryptographic code or algorithms present.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'system reconnaissance' at line 90. Line 90 contains '- hybrid-search-implementation', a skill reference name in a list of related skills. This is not reconnaissance activity.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text 'hybrid-search-implementation' is a skill name, not a network scanning or reconnaissance command.

content.value_statement: This workflow bundle provides a comprehensive guide for building production AI applications, from LLM integration to RAG systems and AI agents. It orchestrates multiple specialized skills into a cohesive development process.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through 7 phases of AI application development
Provide skill orchestration for LLM, RAG, agent, and ML pipeline development
Offer copy-paste prompts for invoking specialized skills
Include development checklists for LLM integration, RAG systems, and AI agents
Define quality gates for AI feature development
limitations: This is a workflow orchestration guide, not executable code
Requires other skills to be available for full functionality
Does not implement AI models directly - it references other skills
Does not include actual code implementations

use_cases.title: Building LLM-powered applications
use_cases.description: Follow the phased workflow to design, integrate, and deploy LLM-powered features with proper observability.

use_cases.title: Implementing RAG systems
use_cases.description: Use the RAG implementation phase to set up vector databases, embedding strategies, and retrieval pipelines.

use_cases.title: Creating AI agent systems
use_cases.description: Design multi-agent architectures using autonomous agent patterns, CrewAI, and LangGraph integration.

prompt_templates.title: Design AI Product Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @ai-product to design AI-powered features for my application. Follow the Phase 1 workflow.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new AI feature project

prompt_templates.title: Build RAG Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @rag-engineer to design RAG pipeline, then @vector-database-engineer to set up vector search, and @embedding-strategies to select optimal embeddings.

prompt_templates.title: Create Multi-Agent System
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @crewai to build role-based multi-agent system, then @langgraph to create stateful AI workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Set up ML Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @ml-engineer to build machine learning pipeline and @mlops-engineer to set up MLOps infrastructure.

output_examples.output: This triggers the ai-product skill to guide you through AI feature design with use case definition, model selection, and architecture planning.
The rag-engineer skill provides guidance on designing retrieval-augmented generation pipelines including data pipeline design, embedding model selection, and vector database setup.

output_examples.input: Use @langgraph to create stateful AI workflows
output_examples.output: LangGraph integration skill helps create complex, stateful AI workflows with proper state management and workflow orchestration.

best_practices: Follow the workflow phases in order for comprehensive AI development
Use the checklist items to ensure all critical components are addressed
Invoke specialized skills for deep expertise in each area
Apply quality gates before deploying AI features to production
anti_patterns: Skipping workflow phases - each phase builds on previous work
Ignoring the observability phase - AI systems need monitoring
Not following security practices - AI features require input validation and rate limiting
Skipping quality gates - AI features need thorough testing before deployment

faq.question: What is the AI/ML Workflow Bundle?
faq.answer: This is a comprehensive workflow guide that orchestrates multiple AI/ML skills into 7 phases: AI Application Design, LLM Integration, RAG Implementation, AI Agent Development, ML Pipeline Development, AI Observability, and AI Security.

faq.question: Do I need other skills installed?
faq.answer: Yes, this workflow references many other skills like ai-product, rag-engineer, crewai, langgraph, and ml-engineer. These skills must be available for the prompts to work.

faq.question: Can I skip workflow phases?
faq.answer: While you can adapt the workflow to your needs, skipping phases may result in incomplete implementation. The phases are designed to build on each other.

faq.question: What tools does this support?
faq.answer: This workflow supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The skill references work across these AI coding assistants.

faq.question: Does this include executable code?
faq.answer: No, this is a workflow orchestration guide in markdown format. It provides prompts and checklists, not actual implementation code.

faq.question: How do I get started?
faq.answer: Start with Phase 1: AI Application Design. Define your use cases, choose appropriate models, and design your system architecture before moving to implementation phases.
